notes: SPRITE: missile.png / empowered.png replace the amethyst-shard look; register as a plain item model, no vanilla enchant-glint (the sprite already glows). It is a four-point arcane spark on the game's own amethyst palette, radially symmetric so it reads from every billboard angle the thrown-item renderer throws at it. Empowered already renders 1.5x, so empowered.png only needs to be a touch bigger in-canvas (wider white core, 8-point cross, faint bloom) — three channels then say 'charged' at once: bigger sprite, brighter/denser trail, layered sound. SOUND LAYERING: empoweredCastSound carries an optional nested 'layer2' — play both SoundEvents at the same position on the empowered cast (max 2 per event, per brief). All other events are single-layer. The plain cast is deliberately ONE soft layer with mandatory jitter so 4 casts/sec shimmer instead of machine-gunning a fixed note; keep volume low. hitSound is new (missiles were silent on impact) — soft crystal tick, jittered because Lance/pierce can land several per tick. VERIFIED 26.2 fields via javap: SoundEvents.AMETHYST_BLOCK_CHIME / AMETHYST_BLOCK_RESONATE / AMETHYST_BLOCK_HIT; ParticleTypes.DUST / END_ROD. DUST maps to new DustParticleOptions(0xRRGGBB, scale) exactly like the existing HOLY_DUST. TRAIL is intentionally a whisper (author removed the old always-on white streak): count 1 every 2 ticks, small, on-line (spread 0), no drift (speed 0) — ~a handful of dim violet specks over the flight, the sprite does the work. If even that fatigues at 500 casts, dropping normal trail to [] is a valid fallback; the sprite stands alone. SEEKER (homing): keep the normal DUST but set everyNTicks 1 (double density) while steering so the curve draws itself — that, not a colour change, is what should read as 'smart'. LANCE (pierce): recolour the existing 6-point END_ROD ring to DUST #B38EF3 so the AOE circle joins the arcane family instead of reading vanilla-white; keep the spinning-ring geometry, it's good.
